title: Infrastructure as Code
summary: Terraform fundamentals, state management, modules, Helm for Kubernetes, and IaC best practices at scale.

sections.content:
## Infrastructure as Code Principles

IaC manages infrastructure through machine-readable configuration files — making infrastructure versioned, repeatable, and auditable. Key tools:

- **Terraform** — cloud-agnostic, declarative, HCL language
- **Pulumi** — IaC with real programming languages (Java, TypeScript)
- **AWS CDK** — AWS-specific, real programming languages
- **Crossplane** — Kubernetes-native IaC using CRDs
- **Helm** — Kubernetes application packaging

sections.title: Terraform Workflow
sections.content:
graph LR
    Write[Write .tf files] -->|terraform init| Init[Download providers]
    Init -->|terraform plan| Plan[Preview changes]
    Plan -->|review| Review{Approved?}
    Review -->|yes terraform apply| Apply[Provision infra]
    Review -->|no| Write
    Apply --> State[Update tfstate]
    State --> Real[Real Infrastructure]
    style Apply fill:#059669,color:#fff
    style Plan fill:#4f46e5,color:#fff

## Helm Charts Best Practices

```
chart/
  Chart.yaml         # version, dependencies
  values.yaml        # safe defaults
  values-prod.yaml   # production overrides
  templates/
    _helpers.tpl     # reusable template helpers
    deployment.yaml
    service.yaml
    ingress.yaml
    hpa.yaml
    configmap.yaml
    NOTES.txt        # post-install instructions
```

```yaml
# _helpers.tpl — define reusable labels
{{- define "app.labels" -}}
app.kubernetes.io/name: {{ .Chart.Name }}
app.kubernetes.io/version: {{ .Chart.AppVersion }}
app.kubernetes.io/managed-by: Helm
{{- end }}

# deployment.yaml — use the helper
metadata:
  labels:
    {{- include "app.labels" . | nindent 4 }}
```

```bash
# Deploy with overrides
helm upgrade --install order-service ./charts/order-service \
  --namespace production \
  --create-namespace \
  --values values-prod.yaml \
  --set image.tag=$GIT_SHA \
  --atomic             # rollback on failure
  --timeout 5m
```

sections.title: Never store Terraform state in Git
sections.content: Terraform state contains sensitive values (passwords, private keys). Use remote backends (S3 + DynamoDB for locking). Add terraform.tfstate and *.tfstate.backup to .gitignore immediately.

sections.question: What is the purpose of a Terraform module?
options: To split large Terraform files into smaller ones for readability
To create reusable, composable infrastructure components that enforce standards and can be shared across teams
To manage Terraform state in multiple environments
To import existing infrastructure into Terraform management
sections.explanation: Terraform modules package infrastructure patterns into reusable components. A platform team creates a 'spring-boot-service' module that includes a Deployment, Service, HPA, and Ingress with all best practices built in. Application teams consume it with just a few parameters. Changes to the module propagate to all consumers, ensuring consistent standards.
